description: Core prescribing cascade knowledge base for clinical decision support
disclaimer: For decision support only. Always verify with current clinical guidelines.

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Prostaglandin inhibition → sodium/water retention + vasoconstriction → BP elevation
differential_hints: Essential hypertension poorly controlled prior to NSAID initiation
White coat hypertension (24h ambulatory monitoring may clarify)
High dietary sodium intake as primary driver
Obesity-related hypertension independent of NSAID use
Renal parenchymal disease causing secondary hypertension
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider if NSAIDs are truly necessary. In PLHIV, prefer paracetamol when possible.
references: Scott IA et al. Med J Aust 2013
Lanas A et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther 2003

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Gastric COX-1 inhibition → reduced protective mucus → GI irritation/ulceration
cascades.ade_en: Dyspepsia, peptic ulcer, nausea, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Helicobacter pylori infection as primary cause of dyspepsia
Functional dyspepsia unrelated to NSAID use
Dietary factors or alcohol contributing to GI symptoms
GERD pre-existing prior to NSAID initiation
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If NSAID is stopped, the PPI may not be needed. In PLHIV verify PPI-ARV interactions.
references: Lanas A et al. 2006
Targownik LE et al. 2008

cascades.name_es: IECA → Tos → Antitusivo / Cambio a ARA-II
cascades.name_en: ACE Inhibitor → Cough → Antitussive / Switch to ARB
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bradykinin accumulation → respiratory tract irritation → dry cough
differential_hints: Respiratory tract infection as cause of acute cough
Post-nasal drip syndrome unrelated to ACEi
GERD-related cough (acid reflux irritating larynx)
Asthma or eosinophilic bronchitis independent of medication
Cough pre-existing prior to ACE inhibitor initiation
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: The correct solution is to switch ACE inhibitor to ARB, not add an antitussive.
references: Dicpinigaitis PV. Chest 2006

cascades.name_es: Antagonista del calcio → Edema maleolar → Diurético
cascades.name_en: Calcium Channel Blocker → Ankle Edema → Diuretic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Preferential arteriolar vasodilation → increased capillary pressure → peripheral edema
differential_hints: Heart failure as cause of ankle edema (require echocardiography)
Venous insufficiency or varicose veins as primary etiology
Hypoalbuminemia-related edema (liver disease, nephrotic syndrome)
Lymphedema unrelated to medication
Deep vein thrombosis (unilateral edema requires exclusion)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider reducing CCB dose, switching to less vasodilatory dihydropyridine, or adding ACEi/ARB.
references: Elliott WJ. J Clin Hypertens 2003

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Glucocorticoid-induced insulin resistance → hyperglycemia
differential_hints: Type 2 diabetes mellitus newly diagnosed independent of corticosteroid
Stress hyperglycemia in acute illness context
Dietary non-adherence as primary driver of hyperglycemia
Other diabetogenic medications (e.g., tacrolimus, atypical antipsychotics)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Review whether corticosteroid can be reduced/discontinued. Insulin is often more appropriate than metformin for steroid-induced hyperglycemia.
references: Clore JN, Thurby-Hay L. Ann Pharmacother 2009

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Osteoblast inhibition + osteoclast activation → bone loss
differential_hints: Primary osteoporosis (postmenopausal or age-related) independent of corticosteroid
Vitamin D deficiency as primary contributor to bone loss
Secondary osteoporosis from hypogonadism or thyroid disease
Malabsorption syndrome reducing calcium absorption
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: May be an appropriate prescription if corticosteroid is needed long-term. Evaluate the context.
references: Weinstein RS. Endocrinol Metab Clin North Am 2012

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Reduced renal urate excretion → hyperuricemia → gout attack
differential_hints: Primary gout with genetic predisposition independent of diuretic
High purine diet as primary driver of hyperuricemia
Renal impairment reducing urate excretion independently
Other uricosuric-blocking medications (cyclosporine, low-dose aspirin)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If thiazide can be stopped, urate levels may normalize. Evaluate indication.
references: Choi HK et al. BMJ 2012

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Activation of mu-opioid receptors in enteric plexus → reduced peristalsis → constipation
differential_hints: Inadequate fluid or fibre intake as primary cause of constipation
Hypothyroidism causing constipation independently
Immobility-related constipation in palliative or post-surgical patients
Irritable bowel syndrome with constipation predominance
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: In this case the laxative may be appropriate. Evaluate if the opioid can be reduced or stopped.
references: Pappagallo M. Am J Surg 2001

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: D2 dopaminergic blockade in nigrostriatal pathway → extrapyramidal symptoms
differential_hints: Idiopathic Parkinson's disease unrelated to antipsychotic use
Essential tremor not caused by D2 blockade
Drug-induced akathisia versus agitation from underlying psychiatric illness
Tardive dyskinesia (late-onset, different mechanism from acute EPS)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Anticholinergics have many adverse effects themselves (confusion, urinary retention, glaucoma). Avoid in elderly.
references: Kane JM et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2009

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonergic activation → central stimulation → insomnia, especially at initiation
differential_hints: Insomnia as symptom of underlying depression not yet responding to SSRI
Anxiety disorder causing sleep disturbance independently
Poor sleep hygiene as primary contributor
Circadian rhythm disruption from shift work or jet lag
Substance use (caffeine, alcohol) interfering with sleep
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Hypnotics carry dependency risk. Consider sleep hygiene, changing SSRI timing, or switching antidepressant.
references: Fava M et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2006

cascades.name_es: Inhibidor de colinesterasa → Incontinencia urinaria → Anticolinérgico
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bladder cholinergic excess → involuntary detrusor contractions → urge incontinence
differential_hints: Overactive bladder syndrome unrelated to cholinesterase inhibitor
Urinary tract infection causing urgency and incontinence
Pelvic floor weakness in older women (stress incontinence)
Benign prostatic hyperplasia causing overflow incontinence in men
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Particularly problematic cascade: treating incontinence reduces effectiveness of Alzheimer's treatment.
references: Gill SS et al. Arch Intern Med 2005

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: GI microbiota alteration and motility changes → diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Irritable bowel syndrome as primary cause of GI symptoms
Infectious gastroenteritis coinciding with metformin initiation
Lactose intolerance or dietary factors driving GI complaints
Celiac disease or inflammatory bowel disease as alternative diagnosis
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Preferred solution: extended-release formulation or dose reduction, not adding an antidiarrheal.
references: Bailey CJ et al. Lancet 1996

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic blockade in enteric nervous system → reduced GI motility → constipation
differential_hints: Inadequate dietary fibre or fluid intake
Opioid co-administration contributing to constipation
Hypothyroidism as independent cause
Immobility in older or frail patients
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Review anticholinergic burden (ACB score). Reduce or switch to agent with lower anticholinergic load rather than adding laxative.
references: Rochon PA, Gurwitz JH. Lancet 1995
Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.name_en: Anticholinergic → Dry Mouth → Saliva Substitute / Pilocarpine
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic M3 receptor blockade in salivary glands → reduced saliva production → xerostomia
differential_hints: Sjögren's syndrome or other autoimmune salivary gland disease
Dehydration or low fluid intake
Radiotherapy-related xerostomia in head-and-neck cancer
Multiple anticholinergic drugs contributing cumulatively (ACB)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess anticholinergic burden. Switch to agent with lower anticholinergic load (e.g., mirabegron for OAB) rather than adding sialagogue.
references: Durán CE et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2013

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Stimulation of chemoreceptor trigger zone (area postrema) and vestibular system by opioids → nausea and vomiting
differential_hints: Nausea from underlying disease (oncology, renal failure) not opioid-related
Constipation-related nausea as secondary phenomenon
Gastroparesis pre-existing independent of opioid
Vestibular cause of nausea (opioid-induced) vs labyrinthine disease
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Antiemetic is often appropriate short-term. Reassess at 2 weeks — opioid-induced nausea typically improves with tolerance. Avoid promethazine in elderly.
references: Benyamin R et al. Pain Physician 2008

cascades.name_en: Opioid → Sedation → Stimulant / Opioid Dose Reduction
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: CNS mu-opioid receptor activation → respiratory depression and sedation
differential_hints: Depression-related fatigue and psychomotor slowing independent of opioid
Anaemia causing fatigue (check Hb in PLHIV)
HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder contributing to drowsiness
Sleep apnoea causing daytime somnolence
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider opioid dose reduction, rotation, or switching to a less sedating analgesic before adding a stimulant.
references: Slatkin NE. J Support Oncol 2009

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Beta-1 blockade → reduced cardiac output + skeletal muscle vasoconstriction → fatigue and exercise intolerance
differential_hints: Heart failure itself as primary cause of fatigue (not beta-blocker)
Anaemia or hypothyroidism causing fatigue independently
Depression with somatic fatigue complaints
HIV-associated fatigue syndrome unrelated to beta-blocker
Deconditioning in sedentary patients
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Assess whether beta-blocker dose can be reduced or switched to cardioselective agent. Exercise rehabilitation may be more appropriate.
references: Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: CNS beta-blockade + possible interference with serotonin and noradrenaline pathways → depressive symptoms
differential_hints: Major depressive disorder pre-existing or independent of beta-blocker
Cardiac disease burden itself causing reactive depression
Chronic pain contributing to depressed mood
HIV-related psychosocial stressors as primary driver
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Evaluate temporal relationship to beta-blocker initiation. Consider switching to hydrophilic beta-blocker (atenolol) with less CNS penetration before adding antidepressant.
references: Ko DT et al. JAMA 2002

cascades.name_en: SSRI / SNRI → Sexual Dysfunction → PDE5 Inhibitor
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonergic excess → 5-HT2 receptor activation + prolactin elevation → inhibition of nitric oxide release → sexual dysfunction
cascades.ade_en: Sexual dysfunction (delayed ejaculation, anorgasmia, decreased libido, erectile dysfunction)
differential_hints: Sexual dysfunction as symptom of underlying depression itself
Testosterone deficiency (common in PLHIV) as primary driver
Vascular erectile dysfunction independent of antidepressant
Relationship or psychosocial factors contributing
HIV neuropathy affecting sexual function
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PDE5 inhibitors + ritonavir-boosted regimens: significant increase in PDE5 inhibitor levels. Start sildenafil at 25 mg max; tadalafil 10 mg no more than q72h.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Discuss dose reduction or switch to antidepressant with lower sexual side-effect burden (bupropion, mirtazapine) before adding PDE5 inhibitor. Check testosterone level.
references: Clayton AH et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2002
DHHS Guidelines 2024

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Increased urinary potassium excretion via sodium-potassium exchange in distal tubule → hypokalaemia
differential_hints: Low dietary potassium intake contributing
GI losses (diarrhoea/vomiting) as primary cause of hypokalaemia
Primary hyperaldosteronism as independent cause
Renal tubular acidosis in TDF-treated patients (VIH002)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Potassium supplementation is often appropriate. Consider switching to potassium-sparing diuretic (spironolactone) or ACEi/ARB if clinically appropriate.
references: Alfonzo AV et al. Nephrol Dial Transplant 2006

cascades.name_en: Diuretic → Dehydration / Orthostatic Hypotension → Falls / Electrolyte Replacement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Excessive volume depletion → reduced cerebral perfusion on standing → orthostatic hypotension → falls
differential_hints: Pre-existing autonomic neuropathy (HIV, diabetes) contributing to orthostatic hypotension
Cardiac cause of syncope requiring investigation
Polypharmacy with multiple antihypertensives contributing
Dehydration from concurrent illness rather than diuretic alone
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess diuretic dose and indication. Orthostatic BP measurement recommended. Consider dose reduction or switch before adding bone protection.
references: Dhruv Kazi et al. JAMA Intern Med 2016

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Anticholinergic + alpha-1 adrenergic blockade → relaxation of detrusor + tightening of bladder neck → urinary retention
differential_hints: Benign prostatic hyperplasia as primary cause in men
Neurogenic bladder from HIV neuropathy or diabetes
Multiple anticholinergic drug burden
Constipation causing external bladder compression
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch antidepressant to agent with lower anticholinergic burden (SSRI, mirtazapine). Treat prostatic disease definitively if confirmed.
references: Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: D2 receptor blockade in tuberoinfundibular pathway → removal of dopamine inhibition → elevated prolactin
differential_hints: Prolactinoma requiring imaging investigation
Hypothyroidism causing secondary hyperprolactinaemia
Other dopamine-blocking drugs (metoclopramide, domperidone)
Renal failure causing reduced prolactin clearance
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch to prolactin-sparing antipsychotic (quetiapine, aripiprazole, clozapine) rather than adding a dopamine agonist.
references: Meaney AM, O'Keane V. Int J Neuropsychopharmacol 2002

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic / Atypical → Weight Gain / Metabolic Syndrome → Antidiabetic / Lipid-lowering
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: H1, 5-HT2C, dopamine D2 antagonism → appetite stimulation + insulin resistance → weight gain and metabolic syndrome
cascades.ade_en: Weight gain, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidaemia, metabolic syndrome
differential_hints: Pre-existing type 2 diabetes or dyslipidaemia before antipsychotic
INSTI-associated weight gain in HIV-positive patients on antipsychotics
Sedentary lifestyle and caloric excess as primary driver
Corticosteroid co-administration contributing to metabolic effects
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to metabolically neutral antipsychotic (aripiprazole, ziprasidone) before adding cardiometabolic drugs. Monitor BMI, glucose and lipids regularly.
references: Newcomer JW. J Clin Psychiatry 2004

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Blockade of NKCC2 transporter in thick ascending loop → increased urinary magnesium wasting → hypomagnesaemia
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesaemia, muscle cramps, cardiac arrhythmia risk
differential_hints: Dietary magnesium deficiency as primary cause
PPI-induced magnesium malabsorption (particularly with long-term PPI use)
Alcohol misuse causing renal magnesium wasting
Refeeding syndrome in malnourished patients
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Magnesium supplementation is appropriate. Assess concurrent PPI use (double diuretic + PPI hypomagnesaemia risk). Consider dose reduction of loop diuretic if feasible.
references: Ryan MP. Annu Rev Pharmacol Toxicol 1984

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Impaired TRPM6/7 channel-mediated magnesium absorption in intestine due to altered luminal pH → hypomagnesaemia
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesaemia (after ≥3 months of PPI therapy), tetany, arrhythmia
differential_hints: Loop diuretic co-administration increasing magnesium wasting (synergistic)
Dietary deficiency as primary driver
Celiac disease or IBD causing malabsorption
Alcohol misuse contributing to renal wasting
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider whether PPI indication remains valid. H2 antagonist may be sufficient with lower hypomagnesaemia risk. If PPI required, monitor Mg and supplement as needed.
references: Cundy T, Mackay J. Aliment Pharmacol Ther 2011

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-2-delta calcium channel subunit blockade → peripheral vasodilation + fluid redistribution → limb oedema
differential_hints: CCB-induced oedema if patient on both CCB and gabapentinoid (CC004)
Cardiac or venous insufficiency as primary etiology
Hypoalbuminemia from nutritional deficiency common in PLHIV
Lymphedema from prior infection or malignancy
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess indication for gabapentinoid. Dose reduction or switch to alternative analgesic/neuropathic pain agent is preferable to adding a diuretic.
references: Shneker BF, McAuley JW. Ann Pharmacother 2005

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Direct mucosal contact + altered epithelial tight junctions → oesophageal and gastric irritation
differential_hints: GERD as pre-existing condition
NSAID co-administration causing GI irritation
Incorrect bisphosphonate administration technique (not remaining upright 30 min)
H. pylori infection as independent cause of dyspepsia
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Verify correct administration technique first (full glass water, remain upright 30 min). Switch to IV bisphosphonate or quarterly ibandronate if oral intolerance confirmed. PPI adds drug burden unnecessarily if technique corrected.
references: Rosen CJ. N Engl J Med 2005

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Beta-1 blockade reducing cardiac output + penile vasoconstriction; thiazide-induced zinc depletion reducing testosterone → erectile dysfunction
differential_hints: Testosterone deficiency (prevalent in PLHIV, aging men)
Vascular atherosclerosis as primary driver of erectile dysfunction
Depression and psychological factors
HIV-associated autonomic neuropathy
Antidepressant contributing to sexual dysfunction (CC019)
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PDE5 inhibitors contraindicated with nitrates (severe hypotension). In PLHIV on PI/r, dose adjustment required — max sildenafil 25 mg/48h.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Check testosterone, assess cardiovascular risk, and consider switching antihypertensive to agent with less ED risk (ACEi, ARB, CCB) before prescribing PDE5 inhibitor.
references: Baumhäkel M et al. Int J Clin Pract 2011

cascades.name_en: SSRI → Hyponatraemia → Sodium Supplement / Fluid Restriction Review
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: SIADH via enhanced ADH secretion and renal V2 receptor potentiation → dilutional hyponatraemia
cascades.ade_en: Hyponatraemia (particularly in elderly), confusion, falls, seizures in severe cases
cascades.cascade_drug_class: Sodium supplement / Fluid restriction advice / Demeclocycline
differential_hints: Hypothyroidism or adrenal insufficiency as primary SIADH cause
Heart failure, cirrhosis, or nephrotic syndrome causing dilutional hyponatraemia
Diuretic-induced hyponatraemia (often concurrent with SSRI)
CNS disease causing SIADH in PLHIV (CNS infection, lymphoma)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: In most cases, stop or reduce the SSRI. Switching antidepressant class is preferable to adding sodium replacement or demeclocycline. Monitor sodium weekly at initiation in elderly.
references: Fabian TJ et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2004

cascades.name_en: Tricyclic Antidepressant → Orthostatic Hypotension → Fall-related Injury Treatment
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-1 adrenergic blockade → peripheral vasodilation → postural blood pressure drop → orthostatic hypotension → falls
differential_hints: Autonomic neuropathy from diabetes or HIV as independent contributor
Volume depletion from diuretic or inadequate fluid intake
Multiple antihypertensive polypharmacy causing cumulative hypotension
Primary osteoporosis in postmenopausal women or PLHIV on TDF
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch TCA to SSRI with lower orthostatic risk (sertraline, escitalopram) rather than treating fall consequences. Assess bone density in high-risk patients.
references: Woolcott JC et al. Arch Intern Med 2009

cascades.name_en: Corticosteroid → Mood Disturbance / Insomnia → Hypnotic / Antidepressant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Glucocorticoid receptor activation in limbic system + HPA axis disruption → mood instability, hypomania, depression, insomnia
differential_hints: Pre-existing bipolar disorder or major depressive disorder
Underlying disease (malignancy, autoimmune) causing psychiatric symptoms
HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder contributing to mood instability
Steroid dose — mood disturbance dose-related (higher risk >20 mg prednisolone)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Taper corticosteroid to lowest effective dose. Avoid benzodiazepines and Z-drugs when possible. Consider quetiapine low-dose for sleep in steroid-induced insomnia with mood component.
references: Brown ES et al. Psychosom Med 2004

cascades.name_en: Statin → Myalgia / Muscle Weakness → Analgesic / Physiotherapy Referral
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Decreased CoQ10 synthesis + impaired mitochondrial function in myocytes → myopathy ranging from myalgia to rhabdomyolysis
cascades.ade_en: Myalgia, muscle weakness, elevated CK, myopathy
differential_hints: Pre-existing inflammatory myopathy (polymyositis, dermatomyositis)
Hypothyroidism causing myopathy independently
Drug interaction increasing statin exposure (PI/r inhibiting CYP3A4, CC001-related)
HIV-related myopathy or AZT (zidovudine) mitochondrial myopathy
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PI/r dramatically increases simvastatin/lovastatin exposure. Prefer rosuvastatin, pravastatin, or pitavastatin in PLHIV on PI/r.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Stop statin and reassess after 4-6 weeks. If myalgia resolves, confirm statin causation. Switch to low-dose hydrophilic statin (pravastatin, rosuvastatin) rather than adding analgesic long-term.
references: Bruckert E et al. Cardiovasc Drugs Ther 2005

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic → QTc Prolongation → Anti-arrhythmic / ECG Monitoring Burden
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: hERG potassium channel blockade in cardiac myocytes → prolonged QT interval → increased risk of torsades de pointes
cascades.ade_en: QTc prolongation, increased arrhythmia risk (torsades de pointes)
differential_hints: Congenital long QT syndrome as independent risk factor
Concurrent medications also prolonging QT (fluoroquinolones, macrolides, antiemetics)
Electrolyte disturbances (hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia) amplifying QTc effect
Structural cardiac disease independently prolonging QT
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Multiple QT-prolonging drugs are common in PLHIV (ARVs + antiemetics + antipsychotics). Use CredibleMeds or similar QT risk checker.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Correct electrolytes (K, Mg). Switch to antipsychotic with lower QTc risk (aripiprazole). Avoid combining with other QT-prolonging agents. Do not add amiodarone without specialist review.
references: Glassman AH, Bigger JT Jr. Am J Psychiatry 2001

cascades.name_en: Alpha-Blocker (for BPH) → Orthostatic Hypotension → Dizziness Medication
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-1 adrenergic receptor blockade → systemic vasodilation → orthostatic hypotension, especially when co-prescribed with antihypertensives
differential_hints: Concurrent antihypertensive drugs amplifying vasodilation
PDE5 inhibitor co-administration causing profound hypotension
Volume depletion from diuretic or poor fluid intake
Autonomic neuropathy from HIV or diabetes
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Alpha-blocker + PDE5 inhibitor → severe hypotension. Use tamsulosin 0.4 mg if PDE5 inhibitor required; avoid first-line non-selective alpha-blockers.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch to uroselective tamsulosin if using non-selective agent. Review and reduce antihypertensive burden. Antihistamines for dizziness add sedation risk — address root cause instead.
references: Chapple CR. Eur Urol 2004

cascades.name_en: Progestogen-only / Combined Hormonal Contraceptive → Depression → Antidepressant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Progestogen-mediated effects on neuroactive steroids and serotonin metabolism → mood changes, depressive symptoms
differential_hints: Pre-existing depression or psychosocial stressors
HIV-related stigma and psychosocial burden as primary driver
ARV-related CNS symptoms (efavirenz — VIH004) contributing
Vitamin D deficiency in women with limited sun exposure
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Explore temporal relationship. Consider switching to non-hormonal or progestogen-free contraception before initiating antidepressant. Note ARV-hormonal interactions (enzyme-inducing ARVs reduce contraceptive efficacy).
references: Skovlund CW et al. JAMA Psychiatry 2016

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic M1 receptor blockade in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus → impaired cholinergic neurotransmission → cognitive impairment, confusion, delirium
differential_hints: Underlying dementia (HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder, Alzheimer's) worsening independently
CNS opportunistic infection in PLHIV causing acute confusion
Urinary tract infection as precipitant of delirium in frail elderly
Electrolyte disturbance (hyponatraemia — CC030) contributing to confusion
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Stop the offending anticholinergic. Switch to mirabegron (beta-3 agonist, no CNS anticholinergic effect) for OAB rather than adding antipsychotic. Antipsychotics worsen anticholinergic burden.
references: Gray SL et al. JAMA Intern Med 2015

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonin and noradrenaline reuptake inhibition + opioid activity → serotonin excess or lowered seizure threshold, especially with SSRI co-prescription
differential_hints: Serotonin toxicity from SSRI + tramadol combination (must assess concurrent SSRIs)
Epilepsy newly diagnosed independent of tramadol
HIV CNS disease lowering seizure threshold independently
Drug interaction: tramadol metabolism affected by CYP2D6 inhibitors (fluoxetine, paroxetine)
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Tramadol + SSRI/SNRI → significant serotonin toxicity risk. Tramadol + carbamazepine → reduced analgesia (CYP3A4 induction). Tramadol is generally avoided in patients on SSRIs.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Discontinue tramadol and switch to a non-serotonergic opioid analgesic. Adding an antiepileptic without addressing the root cause is inappropriate.
references: Tashakkori M et al. Ann Emerg Med 2009

cascades.name_en: Long-term PPI → Osteoporosis / Fracture Risk → Bisphosphonate / Calcium Supplement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Achlorhydria → reduced calcium and vitamin B12 absorption → secondary hyperparathyroidism + bone resorption; also reduced magnesium (CC026)
cascades.ade_en: Reduced bone density, increased hip/vertebral fracture risk with prolonged use
differential_hints: Age-related osteoporosis in postmenopausal women or older men
TDF-related bone loss in PLHIV (synergistic with PPI) — see VIH002
Vitamin D deficiency as primary contributor
Corticosteroid use as additional bone loss driver (CC006)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess PPI indication — consider step-down to lowest effective dose or switch to H2 antagonist. If PPI required long-term, ensure adequate calcium/vitamin D intake and consider DEXA scan.
references: Yang YX et al. JAMA 2006
Corley DA et al. Arch Intern Med 2010

cascades.ade_mechanism_en: H1 antihistamine blockade + enhanced appetite via serotonin/noradrenaline effects → weight gain → metabolic consequences
cascades.ade_en: Weight gain, impaired glucose tolerance, new-onset type 2 diabetes
differential_hints: INSTI-associated weight gain in PLHIV contributing synergistically (VIH003)
Corticosteroid co-administration as additional metabolic driver
Pre-existing insulin resistance or family history of type 2 diabetes
Sedentary lifestyle and caloric excess as primary contributors
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to weight-neutral or weight-reducing antidepressant (bupropion, fluoxetine) before adding antidiabetic or lipid-lowering agent. Monitor weight, glucose and lipids at 3 and 6 months.
references: Serretti A, Mandelli L. J Clin Psychiatry 2010
